title: Divide whole numbers to get a decimal
description: Divide two whole numbers to get a quotient with a decimal. This exercise uses the standard algorithm to find the quotient.
topic_path: Module 2: Arithmetic operations including dividing by a fraction
Topic C: Dividing whole numbers and decimals
Divide whole numbers to get a decimal

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Write your answer in decimal form.*

$4 \div 20 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$\begin{array}{rr} 
&0.\phantom{0}&\\
20&\overline{\Big)\blue{4}\phantom{.}\blue0} \\
\end{array}$

How many times does $20$ go into $\blue{40}$?

$\begin{array}{rr}
&0.\pink{2}&\\
20&\overline{\Big)\blue{4\phantom{.}0}}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{4\phantom{.}0}}\\
&\orange{0}
\end{array}$

$20$ goes into $\blue{40}$ exactly $\pink{2}$ times, so we write $\pink{2}$ in the quotient.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&0.\pink{2}&\\
20&\overline{\Big)\blue{4\phantom{.}0}}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{4\phantom{.}0}}\\
&\orange{0}
\end{array}$

The remainder is $\orange{0}$ so we have our answer.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Divide.**  
*Write your answer in decimal form.*

$18 \div 15 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$\begin{array}{rr}
&0\pink{1}.\phantom{0}\\
15&\overline{\Big){18}}\blue{0}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{15}}\phantom{0}\\
&\orange{3}\phantom{0}
\end{array}$

Write in a decimal and a zero.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&0\pink{1}.\phantom{0}\\
15&\overline{\Big){18}\phantom{.}{0}} \\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{15}\phantom{.0}}\\
&\orange{3}\blue{0}
\end{array}$

How many times does $15$ go into $\blue{30}$?

$\begin{array}{rr}
&01.\pink{2}\\
15&\overline{\Big){18}\phantom{.}{0}} \\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{15}\phantom{.0}}\\
&30\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{\phantom{0}{30}}\\
&\orange{0}
\end{array}$

$15$ goes into $\blue{30}$ exactly $\pink{2}$ times, so we write $\pink{2}$ in the quotient.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&01.\pink{2}\\
15&\overline{\Big){18}\phantom{.}{0}} \\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{15}\phantom{.0}}\\
&30\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{\phantom{0}{30}}\\
&\orange{0}
\end{array}$

The remainder is $\orange{0}$ so we have our answer.

$72 \div 40 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
$\begin{array}{rr}
&0\pink{1}.\phantom{0}\\
40&\overline{\Big){72}}\blue{0}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{40}}\phantom{0}\\
&\orange{32}\phantom{0}
\end{array}$

$\begin{array}{rr}
&0\pink{1}.\phantom{0}\\
40&\overline{\Big){72}\phantom{.}{0}} \\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{40}\phantom{.0}}\\
&\orange{32}\blue{0}
\end{array}$

How many times does $40$ go into $\blue{320}$?

$\begin{array}{rr}
&01.\pink{8}\\
40&\overline{\Big){72}\phantom{.}{0}} \\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{40}\phantom{0.}}\\
&320\phantom{.}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{\phantom{0}{320}\phantom{.}}\\
&\orange{0}\phantom{.}
\end{array}$

$40$ goes into $\blue{320}$ exactly $\pink{8}$ times, so we write $\pink{8}$ in the quotient.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&01.\pink{8}\\
40&\overline{\Big){72}\phantom{.}{0}} \\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{{40}\phantom{0.}}\\
&320\phantom{.}\\
&\mathllap{-}\underline{\phantom{0}{320}\phantom{.}}\\
&\orange{0}\phantom{.}
\end{array}$

The remainder is $\orange{0}$ so we have our answer.
